definitions.text: A public official whose duty it is to administer the law, especially by presiding over trials and rendering judgments; a justice.
A person who decides the fate of someone or something that has been called into question.
To sit in judgment on; to pass sentence on (a person or matter).
examples: A higher power will judge you after you are dead.

definitions.text: To sit in judgment, to act as judge.
examples: Justices in this country judge without appeal.
